rationale: The subject roof is fully covered with blue tarps, which clearly delineate the roof boundary. The orange bounding box measures approximately 260 px × 245 px (56.9 ft × 53.6 ft). The roof is L-shaped with a main section (~42.7 ft × 25.2 ft) in the upper-left and an attached wing/garage (~51.4 ft × 28.4 ft) in the lower-right, with a rectangular notch (~14.2 ft × 25.2 ft) cut from the upper-right corner. L-shape fill factor ~75% of the 3,050 sq ft bounding rectangle yields ~2,290 sq ft, confirmed by direct polygon summation at ~2,330 sq ft; settled on 2,300 sq ft. The roof is a fully-hipped style (no visible gable ends or rake edges). Two interior valley lines form at the L-junction where the two hip sections meet. Ridge segments estimated from visible tarp ridge lines. Eave perimeter traced around the full outer L-shape boundary. Confidence is moderate-low (0.42) due to the tarp obscuring fine structural details and the complex L-shaped geometry making precise corner locations difficult to pin down.
